title: Sanji Feeds a Starving Enemy
description: On pages 18–21, Sanji feeds Gin despite the danger, revealing that his compassion for the hungry matters more to him than restaurant rules or pirate labels.
scene: Gin arrives broken and starving, while the Baratie cooks judge him as an enemy; Sanji alone treats hunger as something that must be answered.

dialogue.speaker: Patty
dialogue.text: Sanji! errand-boy! Both of you get back to work!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I'm Luffyy! Starting today,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I'll be the errand- boy!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Nice to meet ya!

dialogue.speaker: Patty
dialogue.text: Is Table 6's Dessert still not done!?

dialogue.speaker: Patty
dialogue.text: Who took the order for that!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Wasn't it you!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Can't you see I'm Frickin' busy!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Yeah, busy spacing out!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: The hell did you say to me, Prick!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: temporarily suspended from pirating restaurant on the sea's errand-boy working without pay MONKEY D. LIFFYY

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: If you've got nothing to do, at least wash the damn dishes!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: okie-Dokie.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Where's Sanji?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: He's hitting on the customers in the dining room.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Again!? For God's sake...

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: It sickens me to see that a guy like him is the assistant head chef.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Well what're you gonna do. He has been here the longest...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Do you know the reason why Sanji continues to work here despite him and the owner's tense relationship?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: He's trying to become the next head chef, right? Isn't that obvious?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Hey errand-boy! Just how many Frickin' plates have you broken already!?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Ah, my bad. I forgot to keep count.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: You're apologize for that!?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Alright, enough with the dishes! Just clean up instead!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: You can count on me!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Don't eat the food, you bastard!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Oh my god! That was the main dish!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Delish!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Alright, I'll clean this pot then.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hot!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Just get the hell out of the kitchen!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: You're going to take the orders, you hear?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Just ask what the customers want to eat and come back! You can do at least that much, right?

summary: This part reveals the core of Sanji’s morality: no matter who a person is, he cannot ignore someone starving in front of him.
